heading: Income affected by treaty
label: 894 Income affected by treaty
notes: Amendments1997—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 105–341988—Subsec. (a). substituted “Treaty provisions” for “Income affected by treaty” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Income of any kind, to the extent required by any treaty obligation of the United States, shall not be included in gross income and shall be exempt from taxation under this subtitle.”Pub. L. 100–6471966— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), added subsec. (b), and substituted “affected by treaty” for “exempt under treaty” in section catchline.Pub. L. 89–809

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The provisions of this title shall be applied to any taxpayer with due regard to any treaty obligation of the United States which applies to such taxpayer.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 2 Cross reference
children.text: For relationship between treaties and this title, see section 7852(d).

children.heading: Permanent establishment in United States
children.label: b Permanent establishment in United States
children.text: For purposes of applying any exemption from, or reduction of, any tax provided by any treaty to which the United States is a party with respect to income which is not effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States, a nonresident alien individual or a foreign corporation shall be deemed not to have a permanent establishment in the United States at any time during the taxable year. This subsection shall not apply in respect of the tax computed under section 877(b).

children.heading: Denial of treaty benefits for certain payments through hybrid entities
children.label: c Denial of treaty benefits for certain payments through hybrid entities
A
children.text: such item is not treated for purposes of the taxation laws of such foreign country as an item of income of such person,

children.label: B
children.text: the treaty does not contain a provision addressing the applicability of the treaty in the case of an item of income derived through a partnership, and

children.label: C
children.text: the foreign country does not impose tax on a distribution of such item of income from such entity to such person.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 2 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to determine the extent to which a taxpayer to which paragraph (1) does not apply shall not be entitled to benefits under any income tax treaty of the United States with respect to any payment received by, or income attributable to any activities of, an entity organized in any jurisdiction (including the United States) that is treated as a partnership or is otherwise treated as fiscally transparent for purposes of this title (including a common investment trust under section 584, a grantor trust, or an entity that is disregarded for purposes of this title) and is treated as fiscally nontransparent for purposes of the tax laws of the jurisdiction of residence of the taxpayer.
